frameworks.name: feature binding in visual working memory
frameworks.one_line: spatial location as binding anchor between visual features in VWM

frameworks.name: encoding interference account
frameworks.one_line: same-location sequential presentation disrupts perceptual encoding, not binding

core_question: Is recall of feature bindings in visual working memory selectively impaired when items are presented sequentially at the same spatial location?
key_assumptions: spatial location may serve a privileged binding role in VWM
color and orientation are independently encodable visual features
binding memory can be probed by cued reproduction
key_findings: reproduced original impairment of binding memory in same-location condition with original timing
impairment was modulated by ISI and disappeared at longer inter-stimulus delays
with shape-cued color report, same-location deficits did not selectively affect binding
interpretations: observed deficits are best explained by encoding interference, not a binding-specific mechanism
feature binding in VWM is not necessarily impaired when items share spatial location
